macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf 17 and Review of Prior Distinctions Between Neder and Shevua

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and recap of conceptual issues from prior dafim
Language distinctions between shevua and neder; neder's broader effect on mitzvot
Overview of the new mishna on yud zayin: neder within neder versus shevua within shevua

macro_segments.title: The Mishna's Case of Nazir: One Status Cannot Take Effect on Top of Another

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the example of making two nedirim of nazirut; nedir as a status-changing speech act
The conditional nazir example: 'I will be a nazir if I eat; I will be a nazir if I eat'
Explanation of the gemara's interpretation: second nezirut hovers and awaits the termination of the first
Core principle: ein neder chal al neder and ein issur chal al issur; status cannot overlay

macro_segments.title: Shevua Within Shevua: Obligation Cannot Take Effect on Pre-Existing Obligation

micro_segments.title: The parallel case with shevua: two oaths not to eat the same loaf of bread
Principle of mushba ve'omed mehar Sinai: one oath cannot obligate on top of another
Conceptual analysis: shevua as generating obligation versus as swearing falsely
Shevua to perform an already-obligated mitzvah; the gemara's discussion of lerazuzei nafshei

macro_segments.title: Conceptual Difference: Why Nedir (Status) and Shevua (Obligation) Behave Differently

micro_segments.title: Ein neder chal al neder versus ein shevua chala al shevua: the structural parallelism
Why nedir (status) and shevua (obligation) create different logical constraints
Nezirut as intrinsically time-bound and terminal; hovering as unique feature

macro_segments.title: Rav Huna's Position: Nezirut Can Only Overlap if Non-Coextensive

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna: second nezirut takes effect only when explicitly distinguished (today vs. tomorrow)
Demigo deka mitosef yoma yetira: because the second adds an extra day
Two interpretations: distinctness versus the Ran's view that one day must actually take effect

macro_segments.title: Shmuel's Position: Nezirut Overlaps Even if Completely Identical

micro_segments.title: Shmuel: even 'I am a nazir today, I am a nazir today' allows second to take effect
Gemara's challenge to Rav Huna: why didn't the mishna specify conditions?

macro_segments.title: Parallel Case of Two Shvuot: Figs Versus Figs and Grapes

micro_segments.title: Gemara asks: why doesn't a second shvua (not eating figs AND grapes) take effect?
Rava's example: eating figs, bringing a korban, then eating grapes is only half-measure for second shvua
Implication: second shvua (figs and grapes) did take effect; but then why different from nezirut?

macro_segments.title: Rav Huna Does Not Hold Like Rava: Shevua Cannot Overlay Even on Non-Overlapping Subjects

micro_segments.title: Gemara: Rav Huna lo sevira lei k'Rava; they disagree on whether figs-and-grapes shvua takes effect
Rav Huna's reasoning: second shvua is meaningless because already cannot eat figs from first shvua
Rava's reasoning: as long as they are distinct, both take effect (nedir model)

macro_segments.title: Case of Two Nezirut at Once: Rav Huna's Concession and the Concept of Simultaneous Acceptance

micro_segments.title: Gemara brings case: someone accepts two nezirut in a single statement

macro_segments.title: Case of Hataras Nedarim: Retroactive Voiding and the Second Statement's Meaning

micro_segments.title: Someone accepts two nezirut, counts first, brings korban, then does hataras nedarim on the first
Result: second nezirut now kicks in for the last thirty days retroactively
Rav Huna's position: case must have been one day vs. tomorrow to avoid contradiction

macro_segments.title: Case of Two Nezirut at Once: Rav Huna's Concession and the Concept of Simultaneous Acceptance (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna agrees: if stated together, the second can hover and take effect later
Distinction: the problem is sequential statements (hareni nazir hayom, hareni nazir hayom), not simultaneous

macro_segments.title: Case of Hataras Nedarim: Retroactive Voiding and the Second Statement's Meaning (continued)

micro_segments.title: The Ran's alternative: even if identical timing, retroactive voiding makes second statement meaningful

topical_tags.term: ein nedir chal al nedir (one nedir cannot take effect on another)
ein issur chal al issur (one prohibition cannot take effect on another)
mushba ve'omed mehar Sinai (already obligated from Sinai)
ein shevua chala al shevua (one oath cannot take effect on another)
demigo deka mitosef yoma yetira (because it adds an extra day)
